info.title: Streamarr Core Server API
info.description: Interface-agnostic API for Usenet search, resolve, and byte-range streaming (BRIEF §6.2).

post.summary: Exchange admin credentials for a short-lived session token.

get.summary: The identity behind the presented bearer token (machine or admin).

put.summary: Atomically assigns contiguous priorities in the supplied full order.

post.summary: Run a `t=caps` roundtrip against the stored indexer, reporting latency.

put.summary: Atomically assigns contiguous priorities in the supplied full order.

post.summary: Connect + AUTHINFO against the stored provider; report achievable connections.

ChangePasswordRequest.description: Body of POST /api/v1/auth/password — admin self-service password change.

budget.description: The global NNTP connection budget shared across all sessions.

inUse.description: NNTP commands currently occupying a connection across all sessions.

CreatedApiKeyResponse.description: Returned once when a key is minted; carries the one-time plaintext token.

DebugSearchRequest.description: Request body of POST /api/v1/debug/search (BRIEF §6.2). Mirrors the /search params.

DebugSearchResponse.description:
Response of POST /api/v1/debug/search: every release including rejected ones, with
parsed fields, per-rule score breakdown and rejection reasons (BRIEF §6.2). Powers
the ranker-tuning view; still never exposes NZB URLs.

host.description: Populated only for the `nzb_host_not_allowed` error: the rejected download host.

indexerId.description: Populated only for the `nzb_host_not_allowed` error: the owning indexer's id.

ErrorResponse.description: Typed error envelope rendered consistently by every endpoint.

GeneralConfigResponse.description: General config as returned by the config API — the TMDB key is masked.

GeneralConfigWrite.description: Write model for general config. Secret Streamarr.Server.Config.GeneralConfigWrite.TmdbApiKey is write-only.

IndexerDiagnosticDto.description: Per-indexer fan-out diagnostics for the debug view.

allowedDownloadHosts.description: Extra hostnames NZB downloads may use besides the BaseUrl host.

IndexerResponse.description: Indexer as returned by the config API — the API key is masked, never plaintext.

IndexerTestResult.description: Outcome of an indexer connectivity test (BRIEF §6.2 /config/indexers/{id}/test).

allowedDownloadHosts.description: Extra hostnames NZB downloads may use besides the BaseUrl host.

IndexerWrite.description: Write model for indexer create/update. Secret Streamarr.Server.Config.IndexerWrite.ApiKey is write-only.

LoginResponse.description: A minted admin session token and its lifetime.

MeResponse.description: Identity of the caller behind the current bearer token (GET /api/v1/auth/me).

MediaStreamInfo.description:
Neutral media stream shape — deliberately NOT Jellyfin's MediaStream schema
(BRIEF §6.2): the plugin maps this onto Jellyfin's model, other front-ends
consume it as-is.

MetricsResponse.description:
Snapshot returned by GET /api/v1/metrics (BRIEF §10-M7 observability): live sessions,
NNTP connections vs the global budget, cumulative bytes streamed, resolve/fallback
counts, search-cache hit rate, and per-indexer latency.

ParsedFieldsDto.description: The raw parser output for a release (BRIEF §7.1), surfaced for tuning.

tripped.description: True while the provider's circuit breaker is open (failover in effect).

ProviderResponse.description: Provider as returned by the config API — the password is masked, never plaintext.

success.description: True when at least one authenticated connection was established.

achievableConnections.description: How many simultaneous authenticated connections we could open (≤ MaxConnections).

ProviderTestResult.description: Outcome of a provider connectivity test (BRIEF §6.2 /config/providers/{id}/test).

ProviderWrite.description: Write model for provider create/update. Secret Streamarr.Server.Config.ProviderWrite.Password is write-only.

QualityDto.description: Parsed quality attributes of a release (BRIEF §6.2 / §7.1).

ReleaseDto.description:
A ranked release as surfaced by `/search`. The NZB URL and indexer API key are
never present here — they stay server-side (BRIEF §6.2).

ReorderRequest.description: Transactional priority order; every current row id must appear exactly once.

ResolveAttempt.description: One release the resolve pipeline attempted, with its health classification.

viaFallback.description: Resolves that returned a release reached via auto-fallback.

autoFallback.description:
When true (the default), a release that resolves dead transparently retries the
next-best release of the same work, bounded, and returns the first healthy one
(BRIEF §10-M7). Set false to get the raw classification of exactly this release
plus a Streamarr.Server.Contracts.ResolveResponse.SuggestedFallbackReleaseId for manual retry.

streamUrl.description: Same-origin relative capability path; null when the release is dead.

suggestedFallbackReleaseId.description:
Next-best release of the same work. Set when the resolved release is dead and
auto-fallback is disabled (or exhausted), so a front-end can still retry manually.

fallbackFromReleaseId.description:
When this response is the result of auto-fallback, the release originally
requested (which resolved dead). Null when the requested release resolved directly.

attempts.description:
The chain of releases the resolve pipeline tried, in order, each with its health
classification — so a front-end can surface exactly what happened (BRIEF §10-M7).

ResolveResponse.description: Response of POST /api/v1/resolve — the exact shape from BRIEF §6.2.

ScoreLineDto.description: One line of the score breakdown (BRIEF §7.3): a rule and its point value.

hitRate.description: Hit / (hit + miss); 0 when no lookups have happened yet.

SearchResponse.description:
Response of GET /api/v1/search — only works with at least one accepted release;
rejected releases remain available exclusively through the debug contract.

SessionResponse.description: One live session as listed by GET /api/v1/sessions.

WorkDto.description: One aggregated work with its ranked releases (BRIEF §6.2 / §7.4).

bearer.description: Machine API key or admin session JWT.

tags.name: ApiKeys
tags.description:
Machine API key management (BRIEF §6.4 / §9.1). The plaintext token is returned only
once, at creation; thereafter only its prefix and metadata are visible. Keys are
revoked (soft-deleted), not hard-deleted, so past issuance stays auditable.
Admin session required — machine keys cannot mint or revoke keys (BRIEF §6.4).

tags.name: Auth
tags.description:
Admin session auth (BRIEF §6.4): username/password login issuing a short-lived JWT that
unlocks everything including /config and /debug, the caller's identity, and admin
password change. Login itself is anonymous; the rest require a valid session.

tags.name: Caps
tags.description:
GET /api/v1/caps (BRIEF §6.2): the categories the configured indexers search and the
providers streaming can draw from — a front-end's view of what this server supports.

tags.name: Events
tags.description:
POST /api/v1/events — ingests playback events from any front-end into SQLite
(BRIEF §6.1 module 7 / §6.2). This is how watch state escapes a front-end's own DB.

tags.name: GeneralConfig
tags.description:
General config GET/PUT (BRIEF §6.2): TMDB key, TTLs, cache sizes, NNTP budget. The
TMDB key is write-only (masked on read, omit-to-keep on write). Scalar changes take
effect on restart. Admin session required (BRIEF §6.4).

tags.name: Health
tags.description:
GET /api/v1/health: anonymous, shallow liveness by default. Credentialed dependency
diagnostics require an admin JWT and are shared/cached to prevent probe amplification.

tags.name: Indexers
tags.description:
Indexer config CRUD + connectivity test (BRIEF §6.2). Secrets are write-only:
GETs return a masked API key; PUT omits-to-keep. Admin session required (BRIEF §6.4).

tags.name: Metrics
tags.description:
GET /api/v1/metrics (BRIEF §10-M7 observability): a JSON snapshot of sessions,
NNTP connections vs the global budget, cumulative bytes streamed, resolve/fallback
counts, search-cache hit rate, and per-indexer latency. Admin-only, like session
listing, because provider/indexer names and circuit state are operational inventory.

tags.name: Profiles
tags.description:
Quality profile CRUD (BRIEF §6.2 / §7.3). The built-in default profile is always
listed and cannot be edited or deleted; user profiles are stored as JSON. No secrets.
Admin session required (BRIEF §6.4).

tags.name: Providers
tags.description:
Usenet provider config CRUD + connectivity test (BRIEF §6.2, DECISIONS.md #6).
The password is write-only: GETs return it masked; PUT omits-to-keep.
Admin session required (BRIEF §6.4).

tags.name: Search
tags.description:
GET /api/v1/search + POST /api/v1/debug/search (BRIEF §6.2). Both run the identical
pipeline via Streamarr.Server.Services.SearchService; the debug endpoint just projects more of
the result (parsed fields, score breakdown, rejection reasons, indexer diagnostics).
Neither ever exposes an NZB URL or indexer API key.

tags.name: Stream
tags.description:
GET /api/v1/stream/{token} — a capability-authorized, Range-capable HTTP byte
stream (BRIEF §3.3): honors `Range: bytes=…` with 206 + Content-Range +
Accept-Ranges, serves the full body otherwise, and seeks anywhere — including
inside RAR-wrapped files. Player-agnostic by contract; no Jellyfin-specific
behavior may ever be added here.
